papers.title: Reframing Through New Minds: How External Experts Unlock Problem Reframing Through Reasoning Logics
papers.abstract: This study examines how external experts assist organizations in reframing problems by applying different reasoning logics during early innovation stages. Research emphasizes the need to interpret environmental ambiguity and reframe problems. External experts contribute by offering unconventional perspectives that help shift how problems are understood, drawing on five reasoning logics: reverse deduction, reverse induction, explanatory abduction, speculative abduction, and moral abduction.
papers.summary_ja: 外部専門家が5種類の推論ロジック（逆演繹・逆帰納・説明的アブダクション・思索的アブダクション・道徳的アブダクション）を用いることで、組織が問題をリフレーミングし革新的な洞察を得るプロセスを明らかにしたエスノグラフィック研究。

papers.title: Co-Design at the Boundary: Understanding the Dynamics of Open Innovation Between Companies and Communities
papers.abstract: Over two decades, firms have built platforms and engaged open innovation communities to improve and customize products through widened participation in the design process. While benefits of involving external co-designers have been documented, how co-design processes unfold over time at organizational boundaries remains poorly understood. This five-year ethnographic study identifies three co-design types (intimate, instrumental, limited) and reveals how driving forces prompt dynamic transitions between them.
papers.summary_ja: 自動車メーカーとオンラインコミュニティの5年間のコデザイン協働を追跡し、「親密型・手段型・限定型」の3形態が環境・組織要因により動態的に移行することを明らかにした、境界研究の最前線。

papers.title: Artificial intelligence, dynamic capabilities, and innovation resilience: The contingent roles of market competition intensity and technological turbulence
papers.abstract: Study examines how artificial intelligence improves innovation resilience via dynamic capabilities, using panel data from 3,958 Chinese firms (2011-2023) with fixed-effect models. Absorptive capability mediates approximately 50% of the AI-to-resilience effect. Market competition intensity and technological turbulence negatively moderate the AI-dynamic capabilities relationship, suggesting environmental conditions critically shape AI's innovation benefits.
papers.summary_ja: 中国企業3,958社の2011〜2023年パネルデータを用い、AIが吸収能力を主経路としてダイナミック・ケイパビリティを強化しイノベーション・レジリエンスを高めること、ただし競争激化・技術激変環境ではその効果が減弱することを大規模実証した研究。

papers.title: Artificial intelligence driven dynamic capabilities: unpacking micro-foundational mechanisms
papers.abstract: As AI becomes integrated into organizational processes, SMEs face unique challenges and opportunities in developing dynamic capabilities for digital transformation. Study draws on 51 interviews from 28 Finnish SMEs to unpack micro-foundational mechanisms of AI-driven dynamic capabilities, identifying strategic leadership framing, informal team learning, and human-AI trust norms as key components.
papers.summary_ja: フィンランドSME28社51名へのインタビューを通じ、戦略的リーダーシップの枠組み・非公式チーム学習・人間-AI信頼規範の3要素がダイナミック・ケイパビリティの微視的基盤を形成することを質的に明らかにした研究。

papers.title: Digital transformation through innovation: The human-AI decision spectrum
papers.authors: Yufeng Zhang, Xiaojun Wang, Shlomo Y. Tarba, Andy Neely
papers.abstract: This study conceptualizes how humans and AI share decision-making responsibilities in digital transformation contexts, presenting a spectrum model for understanding the division of cognitive labor between human and AI agents in innovation processes. The framework provides a typology of human-AI decision arrangements from fully human-directed to fully AI-directed, with implications for organizational design and innovation management.
papers.summary_ja: デジタルトランスフォーメーションにおいて人間とAIがどのように意思決定を分担・補完し合うかを「スペクトラム」として概念化し、イノベーション管理への含意を論じた論文。著者陣にはTarba（戦略的提携）・Neely（パフォーマンス測定）という世界的権威が名を連ねる。

papers.title: Does AI catalyze novelty? Generative AI, resource allocation novelty, and innovation quality of high-performing manufacturing enterprises in emerging markets
papers.authors: Xingwu Luo, Dejian Yu, Dan Zhou
papers.abstract: Empirical study examining how generative AI catalyzes resource allocation novelty and improves innovation quality in high-performing manufacturing enterprises in emerging markets. Using affordance theory and resource-based view, finds that generative AI's primary contribution is not idea novelty per se but enabling novel patterns of resource configuration that drive downstream innovation quality.
papers.summary_ja: 生成AIが新興市場の高業績製造企業においてリソース配分の新規性を高め、イノベーション品質を向上させるかを実証した研究。GenAIの産業利用に関する実証的エビデンスの先駆け的論文。

papers.title: Disruptive sustainability through digital innovations: Overcoming information asymmetries to benefit small-scale producers
papers.authors: Camille Meyer, John M. Luiz, Anton Grutter, Hamieda Parker
papers.abstract: Study examines how digital innovations overcome information asymmetries that disadvantage small-scale producers, enabling disruptive sustainability pathways and transformative change in established markets. Demonstrates mechanisms by which digitally-enabled transparency and connectivity can redistribute value and power in supply chains to benefit previously marginalized actors.
papers.summary_ja: デジタルイノベーションが小規模生産者に対する情報非対称性を克服し、サステナビリティを「破壊的」に推進するメカニズムを論じた、Research Policy掲載の戦略的研究。

papers.title: AI & The Oracle: Future Interfacing Technical Objects and the Reflexive Practitioner in Futures Studies
papers.abstract: This paper examines implications of integrating AI within Futures Studies methodologies. Juxtaposing divinatory practices—the Delphi Oracle and Tarot—with contemporary approaches like scenario planning challenges the Modern assumption that methodologies are exclusively rational. Drawing on Simondon and Heidegger, argues that AI constitutes a 'technical object' that embeds values and biases, requiring reflexive practitioner awareness.
papers.summary_ja: AIをフューチャーズスタディの「神託（Oracle）」として批判的に検討し、Gilbert SimondonとHeideggerの哲学的枠組みを用いて、実践者がAIとの関係を再帰的に再構成する必要性を論じた思索的論文。

papers.title: From Organizational Resilience Capability to Firm Performance: A Time-Lagged Investigation
papers.authors: Yi-Ying Chang, Susanne Durst, Mathew Hughes, Sascha Kraus, Thomas Clauss, Che-Yuan Chang
papers.abstract: Organizational resilience capability is essential for firms to adapt in an ever-changing world characterized by crises and adversities. The mechanisms and contingencies needed to harness and channel resilience remain uncertain. This time-lagged study of Taiwanese firms shows resilience capability drives both incremental and radical innovation, which in turn improves firm performance, with strategic HRM moderating these relationships.
papers.summary_ja: 台湾企業を危機前後で調査した縦断研究により、組織レジリエンス能力は漸進的・急進的イノベーションを媒介として業績に寄与し、戦略的人材管理がその関係を強化することを実証。

papers.title: A systematic review of design thinking implementations in higher engineering education
papers.abstract: Systematic review examining design thinking implementations in higher engineering education contexts. Analyzes research patterns, application outcomes, challenges, and best practices across multiple institutional settings. Identifies gaps in current understanding of design thinking pedagogy in engineering disciplines and provides recommendations for future research and educational practice.
papers.summary_ja: 工学系高等教育におけるデザイン思考の実装事例を体系的にレビューし、導入効果・課題・実践パターンを総合的に整理したシステマティックレビュー。
